goal: Restructure into a package, fix two M1 carry-overs, then add episodic retrieval: past tickets embedded in a vector store and exposed as a tool the model chooses to call. Isolation now extends to the vector store.

rules.isolation_invariant: every customer read — SQL and vector — is scoped to a single customer_id

status_legend.blocked: Cannot continue until a documented blocker resolves.

features.title: restructure into an installable package
features.user_visible_behavior: All application code lives in support_agent/, tests in tests/, with a pyproject.toml and `pip install -e .`. The app, tests, scripts, and verify.sh all still work from the new layout. Behavior is byte-for-byte unchanged.
features.notes: ATOMIC: a refactor can't be half-done and green, so this one feature is legitimately large; verification is the full gate. Move files, fix every import to support_agent.*, move DB to data/support.db (db_setup creates data/), update check_boundaries.py SCAN/DB_ALLOWED to new paths, update verify.sh (db build = python -m support_agent.db_setup; tests = pytest tests/), add pip install -e . to init.sh and `-e .` to requirements.txt, update CLAUDE.md map. Commit before activating anything else.

features.title: summarizer produces a tight, grounded profile
features.user_visible_behavior: A customer summary is <= 3 sentences, never contains 'Unknown' or '[Not specified]', omits name/plan (those come from columns), and states only ticket-grounded facts — no invented sentiment.
features.notes: Fix is in the summarizer PROMPT, not the mechanism. Tests use the stub summarizer for determinism on length/structure; the no-'Unknown' check runs against a controlled stub output. Assert structure, never exact wording. Manual: regenerate 1001's real summary once and eyeball it.

features.title: close-ticket button in the UI
features.user_visible_behavior: From the app, a user can pick an open ticket, type a resolution, click Close, and the ticket is marked Closed and the customer's summary updates — no terminal needed. A human always performs the close; it is never automatic.
features.notes: Button calls the existing close_ticket(). UI itself is verified manually (record in evidence: closed a ticket from the app, summary changed). Gate just ensures no regression. Closing stays human-initiated by design (D-008 territory: irreversible action = human in the loop).

features.title: vector store with isolated semantic search
features.user_visible_behavior: support_agent/retrieval.py indexes a customer's past tickets into a local Chroma collection (customer_id in metadata, MiniLM embeddings) and search_history(customer_id, query) returns the most semantically relevant past tickets FOR THAT CUSTOMER ONLY.
features.notes: ISOLATION IS LOAD-BEARING HERE: every query passes where={'customer_id': cid}. check_boundaries.py cannot see Chroma, so test_search_isolation (index two customers, assert no cross-leak) is the only guard. Single collection + metadata filter (D-007). Embeddings local via sentence-transformers; first run downloads MiniLM. Chroma persists to data/chroma/.

features.title: retrieval exposed as a tool the model can call
features.user_visible_behavior: search_history is registered as a tool schema. When a ticket references the past, the model emits a tool call; the loop runs the search, feeds results back, and the model answers using them. When a ticket is self-contained, the model answers without calling it.
features.notes: handle_ticket gains an injectable model-call (default = real Groq) so the loop is testable with a stub model that emits a tool call, then a final answer. Tests assert the tool ran and the loop terminated — deterministic, offline. Keep retry-on-429. This is the first REAL multi-step agent loop.

features.title: keep the index in sync + backfill
features.user_visible_behavior: Closing a ticket adds it to the vector index, so future searches can find it. A one-time backfill embeds all existing closed tickets.
features.notes: close_ticket also indexes the now-closed ticket. Backfill is idempotent (re-running doesn't duplicate). Only CLOSED tickets get indexed (consistent with D-004). Tests inject stub embeddings if needed to stay offline/fast.

features.title: surface retrieval, lock the M2 gate
features.user_visible_behavior: The UI/trace shows when retrieval was used for a reply. verify.sh runs the full M1+M2 suite and exits 0.
features.notes: Manual evidence: one trace showing a tool call and one ticket where the model chose NOT to retrieve (the visible judgment — the M2 demo beat). Decide here whether cloud deployment ships local embeddings or a fallback (torch is heavy on Streamlit free tier).
